title: email_list_mailboxes is slow on a cold call (~20s) — design for it, don't treat it as a hang
summary: email_list_mailboxes reads the provider org-accounts list live, which is paginated at the provider — cold it takes ~20 seconds; the server then caches the result ~90s so warm calls are fast. The MCP tool sets a generous 45s client timeout specifically so a healthy-but-slow read isn't aborted (LIST_MAILBOXES_TIMEOUT_MS = 45_000 in packages/mcp-tools/src/admin/email.ts). Two failure modes for naive agents: (1) treating a 15-20s response as a hang and retrying — which just starts another cold read; (2) calling the list per-mailbox inside a loop (audit/enable-imap/rotate flows) — minutes of wasted wall-clock when ONE list call returns the whole annotated inventory. The right pattern is always: one list call → filter/batch in memory → act. Every other tool's account_id + zuid come from this single list, so there's no reason to re-list per mailbox.
references: PR #2041 — Email Admin S2.1 (the email_* tool family + the 45s list timeout)
MEMORY email-admin-initiative — ~20s cold list latency, server-cached 90s, opt-in Smartlead
